rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER GOSK VS. ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 24, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE, INC. DBA ACADEMY OF ART's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is granted, with leave to amend. When Plaintiff filed her First Amended Complaint, Plaintiff exceeded the clear terms of the Order Granting Plaintiff's Motion to Amend Complaint to Name Doe Defendant, filed February 7, 2013. If Plaintiff sought to do more than name a Doe defendant, she was required to give notice of such amendments, attach a proposed pleading, and comply with Rule of Court 3.1324. That did not happen in this instance. rulings.Case Title: PROFESSIONAL COLLECTION CONSULTANTS (PCC) VS. ROBERT M. LUJAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 24, 2013, Line 9, CROSS DEFENDANTs TODD SHIELDS, CLARK GAREN, WIRELESS RECEIVABLES ACQUISITION GROUP & PROFESSIONAL COLLECTION CONSULTANTS's (PCC) DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled and the motion to strike is denied. The Court denies PCC's request for judicial notice because it does not cite the precise basis for judicial notice and the attached documents implicate contested factual issues. Mr. Lujan sufficiently alleges the existence of the Cardmember Agreement. The statute of frauds does not apply because there is nothing showing that the contract could not be performed within a year by its terms. Delaware law appears to apply to this action based on par. 18 of the contract. PCC fails to show that federal law or tolling applies. Mr. Lujan alleges sufficient facts to hold the individuals and Wireless liable and a violation of B&P 6129. CC 1714.10 does not apply to this case based on the allegations in the amended cross complaint. See CC 1714.10(c). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL MATHEWS VS. CLIVE ARMOUR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Relief Under Ccp 473(A)(1) To File Pltf'S Fifth Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 24, 2013, Line 10, PLAINTIFF PAUL MATHEWS' Motion For Relief Under Ccp 473(A)(1) To File Plaintiff'S Fifth Amended Complaint. Plaintiffs' motion for leave to file a Fifth Amended Complaint is granted pursuant to CCP §473(a) on account of counsel's mistake. On March 18, 2013, the Court granted Defendants' motion to strike punitive damages from the Third Amended Complaint, with leave to amend. (See Declaration of Choy, Exh. A.) In Plaintiff's counsel's declaration, he states that he inadvertently filed the Fourth Amended Complaint with incorrect attachments to the Judicial Counsel pleading forms. (Declaration of Bravo in Support of Motion for Relief, 3-4.) However, this order does not address the substance of Plaintiff's proposed Fifth Amended Complaint. Any challenge to the pleadings in the Plaintiff's Fifth Amended Complaint should be brought by a properly filed motion. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTIAN ROETTGERS VS. AUTONET MOBILE, INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 24, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT AUTONET MOBILE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Autonet's general demurrer to Plaintiff Roettgers' First Amended Complaint is overruled as to the first, third, and fourth causes of action, and sustained as to the second cause of action. The general demurrer to the first cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has sufficiently pled the elements of a cause of action for wage violations under Labor Code Sections 201 and 202. The general demurrer to the second cause of action for failure to provide accurate wage statements under Labor Code Section 226 is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given 15 days to plead facts, in good faith, that demonstrate an injury arose from the missing/inaccurate information in Autonet's wage statements. See Price v. Starbucks Corp, 192 Cal. App. 4th 1136, 1142-43 (2011). The general demurrer to the third cause of action for violation of Business and Professions Code Section 17200 et seq. is overruled as Plaintiff has sufficiently pled facts regarding Autonet's alleged unfair and unlawful business practices. See Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc., 14 Cal. App. 4th 612, 619, (1993). The general demurrer to the fourth cause of action for breach of contract and/or breach of covenant of good faith is overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts that Autonet breached its oral promise to Plaintiff to repay his deferred salary if he continued to work for Autonet on a reduced salary through its liquidity crisis. (FAC 8, 25.) Plaintiff's special demurrer is overruled as to the first, third, and fourth causes of action. As to the first and third causes of action, Plaintiff is not required to allege the existence of a contract at the pleading stage to overcome a demurrer. As to the fourth cause of action, it can be ascertained from the First Amended Complaint that the alleged contract between Roettgers and Autonet was an oral agreement. (FAC 8, 25.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TERESA DAVIS, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN VS. ST. MARY'S MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 24, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANTs PAR ANESTHESIOLOGY MEDICAL CORPORATION & MERTON GOODE, M.D.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. The complaint pleads facts sufficient to state a claim for heightened remedies un the Elder Abuse Act (W&I Code § 15610, et. seq.) Ms. Davis has pled sufficient facts to allege a claim for dependent adult abuse by alleging reckless conduct. (Carter v. Prime Healthcare Paradise Valley LLC (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 396, 406-07.)" 'Recklessness' refers to a subjective state of culpability greater than simple negligence, which has been described as a 'deliberate disregard' of the 'high degree of probability' that an injury will occur. [Citations.]" (Delaney v. Baker (1999) 20 Cal.4th 23, 31-32.) Ms. Davis alleges that Dr. Goode engaged in reckless conduct by falsely stating to a nurse and a code blue team member that Dr. Goode had not given the decedent any medications prior to finding him unresponsive. (First. Am. Complaint, 19, 26, 27.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WHITE OAK GLOBAL ADVISORS, A DELAWARE LIMITED VS. TSC ACQUISITION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 24, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANTs TSC ACQUISITION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION & GEMINI PARTNERS, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to the fraud cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff alleges the claim with sufficient particularity. Defendant Gemini's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the breach of contract, breach of implied covenant, declaratory relief, and specific performance causes of action. Plaintiff concedes that Gemini is not a signatory to the contract. See Oppenheimer v. General Cable Corp. (1956) 143 Cal.App.2d 293. The demurrer is overruled as to the fraud and negligent misrepresentation cause of action. Gemini can be liable for its own torts and/or participating with TSC in the commission of a tort. In addition, an agent can be personally liable where its acts are wrongful. See CC 2343(3). Defendants ask for judicial notice of the "November Summary" agreement and Plaintiff does not oppose. The parties agree that Gemini is not a signatory to the November Summary. The Court takes judicial notice of this fact, although neither party has provided a copy of the Summary in connection with this demurrer. See Ascherman v. General Reinsurance Group, 183 Cal. App. 3d 307, 310 (taking judicial notice of contract where there was no dispute over the existence of the contract alleged in plaintiff's complaint). In the future, the parties must provide the Court a copy of any document for which it requests judicial notice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: AFE URBAN, INC. VS. BOOKER T WASHINGTON COMMUNITY SERVICES CENTER, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Response And Production Of Docs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 24, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF AFE URBAN, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Response And Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STARBUCKS COFFEE COMPANY, A WASHINGTON CORPORATION VS. PERSONALITY HOTELS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Form Interrogatories; And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 24, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFF STARBUCKS COFFEE COMPANY, A WASHINGTON CORPORATION's Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Form Interrogatories; And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GRACE JIN VS. CHESTER PAKCHANG CHIU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Pltf'S Claim For Exemplay And Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 24, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANTs JIMMY WONG & WILLIAM WONG's Motion To Strike Plaintoiff'S Claim For Exemplay And Punitive Damages. The motion to strike Plaintiff's claim for exemplary and punitive damages as to Jimmy Wong and William Wong is granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: OMNIMAGNET, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. JEFFREY L. GOODMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Action;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 24, 2013, Line 20, PLAINTIFF OMNIMAGNET, LLC's Motion To Transfer Action. Continued to July 23, 2013 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than June 24, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)
